questions.questionText:
The back-to-back stem-and-leaf diagram shows the ages of members of two sports clubs.

```
         Club A  | Stem |  Club B
           8 5 1 |  1   |  2 4 6
       9 7 4 2 0 |  2   |  1 3 5 8
         6 3 1 1 |  3   |  0 2 7
             5 2 |  4   |  1
```
Key: 1 | 2 | 4 means 21 in Club A and 24 in Club B.

For Club B, find
(a) the mode,
(b) the median age,
(c) the range.

markScheme.desc: (a) No mode (all values appear once) or 'none'

questions.explanation: Read the right-hand side left-to-right for Club B. Order the values to find median.

questions.context:
The table shows the distribution of test scores (out of 60) for 30 students.

| Score | Frequency |
|-------|-----------|
| 21–30 | 3         |
| 31–40 | 8         |
| 41–50 | 13        |
| 51–60 | 6         |

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Calculate an estimate of the mean score.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Write down the class that contains the median.

parts.label: d
parts.questionText: A student says the mean score is exactly 44. Explain why this cannot be verified from the grouped data.
parts.correctAnswer: Individual values are unknown; we only know the class intervals, so the mean can only be estimated using midpoints

markScheme.desc: Individual values not known / data is grouped
Mean can only be estimated using midpoints oe

questions.questionText:
Two data sets each have 10 values.

Data set P: mean = 15, range = 8
Data set Q: mean = 15, range = 20

(a) Find the sum of the values in Data set P.
(b) Explain what the difference in range tells you about the two data sets.
(c) A value of 28 is added to Data set P.
    Find the new mean of Data set P.
    Give your answer correct to 3 significant figures.

markScheme.desc: (b) Data set Q is more spread out / has greater variability than P oe
(b) Both have same mean but Q's values are more dispersed oe

workingSteps: (b) Q has range 20 vs P's range 8 → Q is more spread out
questions.explanation: Range measures spread. Adding one value changes both sum and count.
